title: 5 Surprising Protein Foods
setting: Dark marble counter food photography set, consistent dramatic side lighting from the left, violet color grading, dark slate surface, same overhead camera angle, premium food photography studio

scenes.prompt: Dramatic overhead shot of diverse foods on dark marble surface with dramatic lighting, question mark made of protein powder, moody violet and coral accents, premium food photography, 9:16 vertical
scenes.text: 5 foods with MORE protein than you think
scenes.voiceover: Five foods with way more protein than you think.

scenes.prompt: Macro close-up of cottage cheese in artisan ceramic bowl with berries, dark slate background, dramatic side lighting casting long shadows, violet color grading, premium food photography, 9:16 vertical
scenes.voiceover: Number one: Cottage cheese. Twenty-four grams of protein per cup. More than most protein bars.

scenes.prompt: Beautiful shot of edamame beans in dark bowl with sea salt, chopsticks resting beside, moody Asian-inspired dark background, warm coral accent light, 9:16 vertical
scenes.voiceover: Number two: Edamame. Eighteen grams per cup. And it's plant-based.

scenes.prompt: Cinematic shot of lentil soup in premium dark ceramic bowl, steam rising, dark kitchen background with warm coral and violet lighting, professional food videography, 9:16 vertical
scenes.voiceover: Number three: Lentils. Eighteen grams per cup cooked. Plus fiber for days.

scenes.prompt: Person using dark-themed nutrition app to scan a plate of high-protein foods, app showing detailed macro breakdown, warm lifestyle lighting with coral accents, 9:16 vertical
scenes.voiceover: Save this list and track your protein with Cals2Gains. AI does the counting for you.
